text:
The sources discuss various Sadhana practices, which are spiritual or Tantric disciplines aimed
at achieving specific powers (Siddhis), blessings, or states of being. These practices often
involve the invocation or worship of Shakti, the divine power represented by various deities,
particularly forms of the Maha Vidyas, the ten great wisdom goddesses. The sources delve into
the nature, forms, purposes, methods, and potential outcomes of several such Sadhanas, while
also highlighting the inherent risks and the crucial need for proper guidance.
Baglamukhi Sadhana
• Introduction and Form: Mata Baglamukhi is the eighth Mahavidya. She is also known as
Mata Pitambara, and her favourite colour is yellow. She embodies the power of
Stambhan (paralysis or immobilisation). All vibrations in the universe are attributed to
her. She is among the most famous, prevalent, and effective of the 10 Mahavidya
Sadhanas. She is described as beautiful with a gentle smile. She is three-eyed (Trinetra)
and wears a half-moon (Ardha Chandra) on her forehead. Her body is yellow, and she
wears yellow clothes and a garland of yellow flowers. Her other ornaments are also
yellow, studded with precious stones. She

body is yellow, and she
wears yellow clothes and a garland of yellow flowers. Her other ornaments are also
yellow, studded with precious stones. She wears garlands made of elements related to
the yellow colour, such as Champa flowers and turmeric knots.
• Weapons and Purpose: She rides a jewelled chariot and destroys enemies. In her left
hand, she pulls and holds the tongue of the enemy, while in her right hand, she holds a
mace (Gada). The enemy is extremely frightened by this. Holding the tongue signifies
that she is worshipped for granting and taking away the power of speech (Vak Shakti). In
some places, she uses a dead body or corpse as her seat. Her purpose in holding
weapons is the destruction of the wicked and the protection of her devotees.
• Other Names and Significance: She is also known as Pitambara Devi. Shri Baglamukhi is
known as Brahmastra. There is no mantra equal to Baglamukhi for suppressing hostile
elements (Ahak Paralok), societal dangers (Dahak Arishta), and pacifying enemies.
Sadhaks have relied on her for a long time. According to the Kath Samhita of the
Yajurveda, Baglamukhi illuminates the ten directions, possesses a beautiful form, and is
the goddess of t

g time. According to the Kath Samhita of the
Yajurveda, Baglamukhi illuminates the ten directions, possesses a beautiful form, and is
the goddess of the universe.
• Origin Story: The origin of Mata Baglamukhi was narrated by Mata Kali herself from her
face. It occurred when the universe was created and the previous seven Mahavidyas had
appeared. A new Vidya (knowledge/power) was needed. The reason for her appearance
was the demon Trigurasura. Trigurasura was clever and wise. He performed severe
penance to Brahma, who was pleased and offered boons. The foolish Trigurasura
misused this opportunity and asked for two boons: 1) the power to create a cosmic
whirlwind (Brahmandi Bhra) and 2) that no Vidya in this world, not even the power of
the Trimurti (Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva), could end him. After gaining these boons, he
became arrogant and decided to enslave the Trimurti. He used his power to create a
huge cosmic whirlwind. This whirlwind began swallowing meteorites and planets,
causing storms everywhere, and it grew larger over time.

• Role in Trigurasura's Defeat: Lord Vishnu became concerned and meditated on Lord
Shiva, who told him that only Shakti could stop this destruction. Vishnu and Brahma
went to Hariidra Sarovar and prayed to Devi Maha Tripura Sundari. Pleased, Maha
Tripura Sundari appeared and told them she knew the reason for their arrival. From her
face, Devi Baglamukhi appeared. Devi Baglamukhi, following Tripura Sundari's
command, prepared to fight Trigurasura. First, she used Mohan Vidya on him,
enchanting him. Then she used Stambhan Vidya to immobilise the giant whirlwind,
freeing the planets and stars. Mohan Vidya confused Trigurasura's intellect. Devi then
used Uchchatan Vidya, causing Trigurasura to wander like a madman. Finally, she killed
Trigurasura, freeing the world from crisis.
• Mohan, Stambhan, Uchchatan Vidya: These Vidyas can be used to control the intellect
of even a very strong enemy, immobilise their mind, and destroy them. This Vidya
attacks a person at a mental level. However, it is warned that misusing this Vidya to
torment innocent beings or use it against nature results in a very painful end for the
practitioner. Its use is considered proper only for the protection of Dharm

cent beings or use it against nature results in a very painful end for the
practitioner. Its use is considered proper only for the protection of Dharma.
• Sadhana Method: For Baglamukhi Sadhana, choose a clean place (home, temple, or
other suitable clean location). Wear yellow clothes and use a yellow mat (Asana) during
Sadhana. Use a turmeric mala (rosary) for mantra recitation. Offer lamps on a pile of
yellow turmeric. Offering yellow clothes on the Devi's idol destroys even the greatest
obstacles.
• Benefits and Purpose in Sadhana: Baglamukhi Devi's mantras destroy sorrows. During
worship, do not ask for inappropriate things that are against societal welfare. The
purpose of your Sadhana or worship should not be to harm anyone. Wish only for what
is just and universally loved. Baglamukhi Vidya can be used for achieving both Bhoga
(worldly enjoyment) and Moksha (liberation). By worshipping her, any rare thing can be
obtained. However, she is especially effective (Amogha) for Stambhan, enemy
pacification (Shatru Shaman), victory in war, arguments, court cases, and
competitions. A person worshipping this power can cause desired suffering to their
enemy and also remove their own suff

r, arguments, court cases, and
competitions. A person worshipping this power can cause desired suffering to their
enemy and also remove their own suffering.
• Mantra: The special mantra of Mata Baglamukhi is: "Om Hleem Baglamukhi Sarva
Dushtanam Vacham Mukham Padam Stambhaya Jivham Keelaya Buddhim Vinashaya
Hleem Om Svaha". This mantra should be chanted daily according to one's capacity. Use
a turmeric mala for chanting. Perform general worship of Mata Baglamukhi before
chanting. By this, enemies are pacified, tasks are quickly accomplished, and Devi's grace
remains. If someone is not getting success in any work, they should chant this mantra. It
is excellent for bringing success. Another mantra is "Om Hreem Hreem Baglamukhi
Devyai Hleem Svaha".
Dhumavati Sadhana
• Introduction and Significance: Dhumavati Sadhana holds a significant place among the
10 Mahavidyas. Two points are noteworthy: it is a special power of Maa Durga for
resolving conflict (Kalah Nivarni Shakti), and it is Parvati's vast and protective (Raksha)
form. Dhumavati is considered supreme among the 10 Mahavidyas in the field of
is considered supreme among the 10 Mahavidyas in the field of

Sadhana. Where Dhumavati Sadhana is performed, it results in enemy destruction and
obstacle removal. Dhumavati Sadhana is primarily a Tantric Sadhana. Upon Siddhi
(perfection) of this Sadhana, ghosts, spirits (Pishach), and other Tantric obstacles do not
affect the Sadhak or their family; instead, even the strongest obstacles are resolved.
• Form and Purpose: Dhumavati's form is described as distressed by hunger (Kshudha
Pidit). She is black-वर्ण (Krishna Varni). She is fearless (Abhaya) towards her devotees and
the incarnation of Kala (time/death) for their enemies. She requires something for
consumption. When a Sadhak performs this Sadhana, Devi is pleased and consumes
their enemies, granting the Sadhak fearlessness.
• Benefits: Achieving Siddhi in Dhumavati Sadhana is considered a great fortune for a
Sadhak. Those who wish to move forward in life fearlessly and without attachment
should perform this Sadhana. Upon Siddhi, the Sadhak receives numerous benefits. The
Sadhak's body becomes strong and firm. They are less affected by heat, cold, hunger,
thirst, or illness. Upon Siddhi, Agni Deva (fire god) resides in the Sadhak's eyes. By
looking intensely at an enemy and mentally chan

, cold, hunger,
thirst, or illness. Upon Siddhi, Agni Deva (fire god) resides in the Sadhak's eyes. By
looking intensely at an enemy and mentally chanting the Dhumavati mantra, the enemy
is instantly defeated. Siddhi brings strong hypnotism (Sammohan) and attraction power
to the Sadhak's eyes. Bhagwati Dhumavati protects the Sadhak. Even if the Sadhak is
alone in difficult situations or among enemies, they remain unharmed; the enemies
themselves become ineffective and powerless. This Sadhana allows the Sadhak to defeat
their enemies, retaliate against Tantra attacks in kind, and crush their pride. Like Tara is
associated with prosperity/intellect and Tripurasundari with valour/fortune, Dhumavati
is the Devi who strikes enemies like a fierce thunderbolt. She grants rapid power and
strength to her worshipper, helping the Sadhak directly and indirectly. Worldly comforts
require the strength gained through Sadhana to remain safe from enemies and progress.
In today's world, close people often plot against you. Dhumavati Sadhana is the only way
to protect yourself from this. Without it, progress is difficult. It can help when problems
start unexpectedly in a family, illness doesn't respo

way
to protect yourself from this. Without it, progress is difficult. It can help when problems
start unexpectedly in a family, illness doesn't respond to medical treatment, and it is
revealed that someone has performed black magic (Tona Totka). Dhumavati is the
"Darun Vidya" (fierce knowledge). There is no better solution for removing burning
poverty (Dahak Daridrya), worries, and obstacles. The great Devi, being pleased, not only
consumes all the Sadhak's enemies but also ensures they lack nothing in wealth and
grain because this Sadhana causes Maa Bhagwati Dhumavati to consume obstacles to
gaining Lakshmi (wealth). Therefore, this power should also be worshipped for wealth
acquisition. It is an unfailing Sadhana that turns misfortune into good fortune. Its effect
is said to be more certain than a bullet. Dhumavati Sadhana is the seventh Mahavidya
(contradicts earlier source listing Baglamukhi as eighth, but both are part of the same
group). She is the great power that destroys enemies and removes sorrows. Through this
Sadhana, the Sadhak gains the power to defeat evil forces and turn adverse situations
favourable. Mastering time is possible through Shakti Sadhana, which allows

Sadhak gains the power to defeat evil forces and turn adverse situations
favourable. Mastering time is possible through Shakti Sadhana, which allows misfortune
to be changed into good fortune.
• Sadhana Method: This Sadhana can be started on Dhumavati Jayanti, any month's
Ashtami (eighth lunar day), Amavasya (new moon day), or Sunday. It should be
performed in a solitary place such as a empty building, cremation ground (Smashan),
forest, cave, or secluded room. It is a night Sadhana and should be performed after 9

PM. First, bathe and wear red clothes. Wear a Guru Pitambar (shawl) and sit on a red
woollen mat (Asana), facing west. Spread a red cloth on a stool (Chowki) in front of you.
• Sadhana Materials (Specific): A picture of Maa Bhagwati Dhumavati, Dhumavati Yantra,
and Kali Hakiq Mala are specifically mentioned as having special utility. If these are not
available, draw the 'Dhum' Bija (seed syllable) with lampblack (Kajal) on a copper vessel,
place a betel nut (Supari) on it, and worship that as Maa Dhumavati. A Rudraksha Mala
can be used for chanting.
• Preliminary Rituals: First, light a pure ghee lamp and incense sticks. Perform general
Guru worship and chant the Guru Mantra four times. Seek permission from the Sadguru
Dev Ji and pray for the Sadhana's unobstructed completion and success. Then, perform
brief Ganesh Puja and chant the mantra "Om Vakratundaya Hum" one time. Pray to Lord
Ganapati for the Sadhana's success without obstacles. Then, perform brief Bhairav Puja
and chant the mantra "Om Aghor Rudraya Namah" one time. Pray to Lord Aghor Rudra
Bhairav Ji for the Sadhana's unobstructed completion and success. After these steps are
complete, the Sadhana will show results in a f

ord Aghor Rudra
Bhairav Ji for the Sadhana's unobstructed completion and success. After these steps are
complete, the Sadhana will show results in a few days.
Matangi Sadhana
• Introduction and Form: Among the 10 Mahavidyas, the ninth Mahavidya is Devi
Matangi. She is described as the mistress of nature. Some of her famous names are
Sumukhi, Laghu Shyamala or Shyamala, Raj Matangi, Karna Matangi, Chand Matangi,
Vashya Matangi, Matangeshwar, etc.. During Navratri, Devi Matangi is worshipped and
propitiated on the ninth day. In this form, Lord Shiva always stays with her in his Matang
form. Devi Matangi is of a deep blue colour. She wears a half-moon on her forehead.
She has three radiant eyes. She is seated on a jewelled throne.
• Attributes and Symbolism: In one hand, she holds a mala of Gunja seeds. In her right
hands, she holds a Veena and a Kapal (skull cup). In her left hands, she holds a Khadga
(sword). Devi Matangi is in the Abhaya Mudra (gesture of fearlessness). She has a parrot
with her, which is considered a symbol of speech and recitation (Vachan). In her four
arms, she holds a Kapal, Veena, Khadga, and Veda (scripture). Maa Matangi is
considered the Saraswati Devi of Ta

and recitation (Vachan). In her four
arms, she holds a Kapal, Veena, Khadga, and Veda (scripture). Maa Matangi is
considered the Saraswati Devi of Tantrics.
• Benefits: We will be told about the Sadhana method and the amazing benefits. Through
Karna Matangi Sadhana, one can know the future. One can feel upcoming events
beforehand. Devi Matangi also destroys the effects of Indrajal (magic) and sorcery. She
is considered the Devi of speech (Vachan), Tantra, and art. This is a Sadhana that gives
results quickly. Maa gives information about future events in dreams. Maa blesses those
who perform Sadhana with selfless devotion. Since results are obtained easily, it is
difficult for the Sadhak to remain selfless. Being a result-oriented Sadhana, its effect
tends to wear off quickly, so the Sadhak should repeat the chanting every three or six
months.
• Sadhana Method and Mantras: Start the Sadhana on Gupt Navratri, Akshay Tritiya, or
Matangi Jayanti or Matangi Siddhi day. Besides this, it can be started on the first day of
any Navratri or any Friday. Akshay Tritiya to Vaishakh Purnima is considered the best
time.

y Friday. Akshay Tritiya to Vaishakh Purnima is considered the best
time.

• Karna Matangi Mantra: "Om Namah Shri Matangi Amogha Satyavadi Mama Karane
Avatara Avatara Satyam Kathayathi Shri Matange Namah".
• Another Mantra: "Om Namah Karna Pishachini Amogha Satyavadi Mama Karne
Avatara Avatara Atit Anagata Vartaman Ni Darshaya Darshaya Mama Bhavishya
Kathayaya Kathaya Hreem Karna Pishachini Svaha".
• Karna Matangi Sadhana Technique: Start the Karna Matangi Sadhana Mantra with the
'Aim' Bija. The method for this specific Sadhana is complex and best learned under the
guidance of a Guru or Siddha Purusha.
• Other Useful Mantras: The source also provides other useful, self-fulfilling mantras that
give immediate benefits. It is advised to chant any Siddha Mantra 1,00,000 times before
using it in practice.
• Shiv Manju Ghosh Mantra: For financial problems, the Shiv Manju Ghosh Uchchista
Sadhana can quickly resolve the crisis. After eating, make a Damaru shape with leftover
food on the plate. Write the mantra "A Ra Va Cha La Dhi" above and below the Damaru
shape with a finger. Chant this mantra 108 times in that position. Continue daily until the
financial crisis is resolved. Many people have benefited from this.

sition. Continue daily until the
financial crisis is resolved. Many people have benefited from this.
• Udar Peeda Nivarak Hanuman Mantra (Stomach Pain Relief): The mantra is "Jo Jo
Hanumant Dhag Jit Phal Phalita Ayura Khura". This mantra is said to be like a रामबार्
(panacea/sure shot). It brings immediate improvement in the condition of a person with
stomach pain. Within 24 hours, the situation improves satisfactorily. Perform daily until
completely healed.
• Method for Hanuman Mantra: Two methods: 1) Chant 108 times while stroking the
patient's stomach downwards. 2) Keep water or Ganga Jal in a vessel, chant the mantra
108 times to energize it, then give it to the patient to drink.
Mohini Vidya / Shiv Mohini Vidya
• Nature and Effect: Mentioned in ancient Tantra texts and Vedas. Described as the power
of Vashikaran (control/attraction) and Mohini (enchantment/hypnotism). It reveals a
mysterious power and leaves a deep impression. It doesn't just affect a person physically
but changes their mental state and thinking. When affected, a person feels completely
hypnotised. Their mental state is such that they completely deny the reality around
them and feel a deep attachment (Aasakti)

person feels completely
hypnotised. Their mental state is such that they completely deny the reality around
them and feel a deep attachment (Aasakti) towards a specific person or object. This
hypnotism becomes more effective when the person, while conscious, becomes
completely absorbed in their desires and actions. The effect is so deep that every
decision and action depends on the will of another, as if someone else is controlling their
thoughts.
• Tools: Tantra Vidya uses Tratak (fixed gazing), Mantra (chanting), and Yantra (geometric
diagrams) for Mohini Vidya. Tratak means gazing fixedly at a point or object without
blinking to focus the mind and consciousness. Mantras intensify the effect, and Yantras
activate the energy. The combination is very powerful for hypnotising someone.
• Historical Context: While precise proof is lacking, its mention exists in the Vedas. Its
principles are deep and influential, found in Tantra, Dharma, culture, and life aspects.

Today, Mohini Vidya is not widely recognised but its history is old and deep. It is still
considered a mysterious and powerful Vidya.
• Lord Krishna as Master: Lord Shri Krishna is described as a skilled and powerful user of
Mohini Vidya. He was a master of Tantra and Mantra and had extensive knowledge of
Mohini and Vashikaran. He used these powers to achieve success in various life aspects.
His Mohini Vidya was so influential that he made many impossible tasks possible, and
stories of his use of Mohini Vidya are still alive. His life proves that when these powers
are used with right purpose and knowledge, they can affect not just an individual but
society and the universe. His use was unique, making him worshipped as the best master
of Mohini and Vashikaran Vidya.
• Mention in Texts: The Rigveda has a shloka revealing Mohini Vidya's power:
"Hastabhyam Dashashakhabhyam Jihva Vachan Puragava Anametamyasa Prash". Its
meaning: "You who are the first inspiration to my tongue, I touch you with my ten finger-
like branches, by which your disease will be removed and health will increase." This is
seen as an example of Mohini practice using speech and hands for healing/increasing
power. Mo

sease will be removed and health will increase." This is
seen as an example of Mohini practice using speech and hands for healing/increasing
power. Mohini Vidya, a powerful form of Tantra/Mantra, affects physical, mental, and
spiritual levels. It is also mentioned in the Durga Saptashati's Kunjika Stotram: "Maran
Mohan Vashya Stambhan Uchchatanadikam Path Matram Sansiddhyat Kunjika
Stotram". Its meaning: "By merely reciting the Kunjika Stotram, powerful Tantra
Sadhanas like Maran, Mohan, Vashikaran, Stambhan, and Uchchatan are perfected.".
• Shiv Mohini Vidya Sadhana: A mysterious and powerful Sadhana mentioned in religious
texts and Puranas. Achieving Siddhi can be difficult today. It can be performed on
auspicious festivals, especially Mahashivratri. It has a powerful result. It can also be
done on a Monday or Tuesday, but the effect is less.
• Sadhana Method: Shiv Mohini Sadhana is a one-day Sadhana. It can be done anytime
from sunrise to the next sunrise. You only need a Rudraksha mala. Achieving success
makes your life blessed. It must be done with faith and belief as it is a power-sensitive
process and can be harmful if misused.

process and can be harmful if misused.
• Cautions: Vashikaran Sadhana should only be used for positive purposes. Misuse to
harm someone is forbidden. It can affect mental and emotional balance, so it should be
done carefully. Guru's guidance is essential for Siddhi in Tantra Mantra, and this
Sadhana should not be done without it.
Kapal Sadhana
• Warning: This Sadhana is described as fierce (Ugra) and mysterious and can be serious
and dangerous. It should only be done under the guidance of experienced Gurus and
drawing on ancient texts. Do not take any step without proper knowledge and
understanding. Such Sadhanas can affect physical and mental balance. The source is not
responsible for any harmful or undisciplined behaviour. Work cautiously and prioritise
your safety.
• Nature and Purpose: Kapal Sadhana is connected to a deep and mysterious religious
practice. It involves the skull (Kapal) of a dead person, specifically the upper part, which
is considered a centre of special power. Through this Sadhana, the Sadhak attempts to
ial power. Through this Sadhana, the Sadhak attempts to

understand and use the energy, powers, and life mysteries of the deceased person. The
skull contains accumulated energy and Sanskar (impressions) of the person's life.
Focusing on the skull and including it in the divine Sadhana process can be a source of
great power and Siddhi.
• Practitioners and Locations: Avghad and Aghori Sadhaks often follow this Sadhana to
gain necessary power for performing fierce and terrifying (Ugra and Bhayankar) tasks.
They specifically use the Kapal Patra (skull cup), believing it helps them control not only
ghosts and spirits (Bhut Pret) but also their divine powers. This Sadhana attempts to
understand the cycle of life and death and connect with the deeper mysterious powers
of life, which often manifest as strange, frightening, and fierce practices.
• Atmosphere and Challenge: Kapal Sadhana is a fierce and terrifying Sadhana performed
in desolate and scary places like cremation grounds (Shmashan or Marghat). The
Sadhak attempts to use the mysterious and terrifying powers associated with the skull.
In the dark night, alone in the dense darkness of the Shmashan, loud noises and fear are
natural. A peculiar aspect is that the more fear experienced durin

e dark night, alone in the dense darkness of the Shmashan, loud noises and fear are
natural. A peculiar aspect is that the more fear experienced during practice, the more
powers the Sadhak gains. This Sadhana is only for those who can maintain mental peace
and remain committed to their objective despite difficulties.
• Siddhi and Duration: Siddhi requires a deep process and takes time. The Sadhana
becomes more intense over time, testing the Sadhak's self-strength and patience. The
more difficult it is, the more powerful and effective the Siddhi, if done with full faith and
dedication. Time may vary, but Siddhi is certain eventually.
• Offerings: During and after the Sadhana, offerings such as Dhoop (incense), Deep
(lamp), Bhog (food offerings - sweets, water, and Madiira/alcohol) should be offered to
Kapaleshwar and the five skulls daily.
• Discipline After Sadhana: It is crucial to remain very disciplined after the Sadhana. The
Sadhak must ensure they do not harm anyone, otherwise the Sadhana is broken, and
suffering increases. This mysterious process requires immense power and a high mental
state.
• Requirements and Materials: Kapal Sadhana is a deep and mysterious Sadhana
requir

mysterious process requires immense power and a high mental
state.
• Requirements and Materials: Kapal Sadhana is a deep and mysterious Sadhana
requiring specific materials and atmosphere. The Sadhak must use these items with full
faith and patience. Specific materials and items used during Sadhana are detailed.
• Sadhana Completion: After completing the Sadhana, the Sadhak should offer water and
Bhog to the skulls daily while meditating and worshipping. The Sadhak needs to live a
fully disciplined and pure life afterwards to avoid suffering or adverse effects. The puja
and Sadhana materials help the Sadhak gain divine powers and Siddhi through the skulls.
It is a fierce and deep Sadhana where success comes only with concentration,
dedication, and patience.
Shakti Sadhana (General)
• Concept and Purpose: Shakti Sadhana is the practice where a person invokes Shakti in
the form of Devi. It is done particularly through the worship of Maa Durga, Kali, and
Lakshmi. Its aim is to awaken the hidden energy and power within a person. It helps
the individual establish confidence, mental strength, and balance in various aspects of
the individual establish confidence, mental strength, and balance in various aspects of

life. It is a deep inner journey connecting to the invisible world of the soul and Brahman.
It develops physical and mental power and fills life with a new perspective and strength.
• Internal Awakening: It is a crucial means of awakening spiritual power. When a Sadhak
performs Shakti Sadhana, they feel deep inner peace and elemental consciousness. It
leads to self-realisation, recognizing one's true nature. This path connects to the
supreme truth of Brahman. It's like descending into the ocean of deep self-knowledge. It
gradually frees the mind and illuminates the soul with divine light.
• Benefits (General and Deity Specific): Worshipping Maa Durga awakens hidden powers
for facing life's struggles and guiding towards union with Brahman. Shakti Sadhana
awakens inner energy and improves physical and mental state. Worship of Durga, Kali, or
Lakshmi allows their energy to enter the Sadhak. Worship of Maa Durga awakens
extraordinary power for physical strength. Worship of Maa Kali provides mental courage
and spiritual strength to face life's difficulties. Worship of Maa Lakshmi opens the path
to economic prosperity and reduces mental stress and worry. This state of mental peace
and pr

fe's difficulties. Worship of Maa Lakshmi opens the path
to economic prosperity and reduces mental stress and worry. This state of mental peace
and prosperity brings balance and happiness. Shakti Sadhana establishes harmony in
body and mind, giving the power to overcome any crisis.
• Experience of Inner Power: A Sadhak absorbed in Shakti Sadhana experiences invisible
inner power, making them self-sufficient and independent of external resources for life's
difficulties. This power strengthens them mentally to overcome any challenge. By
surrendering to Durga, Kali, or Lakshmi, the Sadhak feels no problem is too big with
inner strength and faith. This Sadhana provides power in every aspect of life: physical
health, mental state, social relationships.

success.
o Maa Durga: Symbol of power, courage, struggle. Protects the universe,
represents power against evil/Adharma. Worship gives courage and strength to
face challenges. Involves reciting Durga Saptashati, chanting Durga Kavach,
special fasts. Awakens unique inner power, gives mental and physical strength.

Reciting Durga Saptashati gives power for victory in life.
o Maa Kali: Deep and fierce form of power. Goddess of destruction and

o Maa Lakshmi: Goddess of wealth, prosperity, opulence. Blessings bring

considered very effective. Opens path to material prosperity and promotes
mental peace and balance. Reciting Shri Suktam leads to financial success and a

to live a better life. Prosperity comes not just from wealth but from mental

• Sadhana Method (General): A deep and contemplative process inspiring spiritual
development and filling life with power, peace, balance. Demands discipline and
dedication to connect with divine powers.
o Selecting Location: Crucial to select a quiet and holy place free from distractions.

Maran Tantra Vidya
• Context in Tantra: Maran Tantra is discussed in ancient literature, including the Agni
Purana. The Agni Purana describes the Shatkarma (six actions) in Tantra: Shanti Karma
(pacification), Vashikaran (control/attraction), Stambhan (immobilisation), Vidveshan
(causing enmity), Uchchatan (driving away), and Maran (killing/destruction). It also lists
nine uses of Tantra: Maran, Mohan, Stambhan, Vidveshan, Uchchatan, Vashikaran,
Akarshan (attraction), Yakshini Sadhana, and Rasayana Kriya.
• Methods and Practices: Variou

Maran, Mohan, Stambhan, Vidveshan, Uchchatan, Vashikaran,
Akarshan (attraction), Yakshini Sadhana, and Rasayana Kriya.
• Methods and Practices: Various deities and practices are used. Aghoris use Shiva
Sadhana, Shava Sadhana (corpse worship), and Shmashan Sadhana. Many people use
Bhairav Sadhana, Naga Sadhana, Paishachini Sadhana, Yakshini Sadhana, or Rudra
Sadhana.
• Lower Level Uses: Tantra is also claimed to have lower level uses like removing evil eye
(Nazar Lagna), madness (Unmad), spirit possession (Bhutonmad), planetary ill effects
(Graho ka Anishta), bad days, externally motivated spells (Abhichar), mental agitation. It
is claimed to heal physical diseases, snake/scorpion bites, and poisonous boils.

• Core Purpose: The original aim of Tantric Sadhana is Siddhi (self-realisation), done
through inward-focused practices. There are three paths: Vama Marga, Dakshina Marga,
and Madhyama Marga.
• Definition of Maran: According to Agni Purana, Maran is considered a Nishta Karma
(vile/low act). Maran means causing death or death-like suffering.
• Mechanisms of Maran: This includes increasing the Vayu element (causing memory loss,
buzzing in ears, nerve/bone pain/disorder), increasing the Pitta element (disrupting
digestion, causing boils, gangrene, cancer), increasing the Kapha element (causing
asthma, bronchitis, cough). Agni Purana has many uses for Maran, Mohan, Uchchatan.
Maran is used for destroying enemies.
• Types of Maran:
o Instant Death: Practices like Ghat or Kratya are famous. These are powerful
Tantric fire-weapons, invisible but strike like a bullet to kill the enemy.
o Slow Maran (Manda Maran): Practices to make someone ill. Can cause diseases
like fever, diarrhea, pain, paralysis, madness, delusion, similar to germ warfare.
• Possibility and Ethics: The sources state that while ethics are a separate question, such
practices are possible, not impossible. Just as poison o

• Possibility and Ethics: The sources state that while ethics are a separate question, such
practices are possible, not impossible. Just as poison or weapons can kill, there can be
invisible means (like fatal rays) that concentrate nature's destructive particles to attack
and kill a target. Or direct disease germs to a specific person. Ancient curses (Shrap)
were similar, concentrating spiritual powers. Tantric Abhichara can also harm others.
• Cost to Practitioner: It is important to note that such practices significantly deplete the
practitioner's power. They require extraordinary practices to replenish it, like a mother
after childbirth or a snake after biting.
• Countering Maran: Just as Tantra can be used for Maran, Mohan, Uchchatan, a skilled
Tantric can also stop these practices, neutralize them, or even reverse the attack back
onto the practitioner. Reversing practices like Ghat, Kratya, Chowki onto the practitioner
can cause immense disaster for them.
• Ethical Prohibition and Secrecy: Such harmful practices happen and are possible
through Tantra Vidya. However, from the perspective of ethics, dharma, humanity, and
divine law, they are absolutely improper and undesirable

possible
through Tantra Vidya. However, from the perspective of ethics, dharma, humanity, and
divine law, they are absolutely improper and undesirable. If such secret killings became
common, it would cause great chaos, destroy goodwill and trust, and make everyone
suspicious of others. Therefore, Indian Tantrics declared these practices forbidden and
kept their methods secret. Like the secrets of atomic bombs are kept secret to prevent

• Mechanism Involving Spirits: When someone uses Maran Tantra, they first need to find

death). For example, to kill someone in an accident, the Tantric needs to redirect a spirit
from an accident towards the target using a mantra. If someone died by vomiting blood,
redirecting that spirit means the target will also die by vomiting blood. If someone
hanged themselves, redirecting that spirit means the target will also die by hanging.
• Symptoms of Maran Tantra Attack: The body resists negative energy attacks. The
heartbeat becomes faster than normal. The deadlier the attack, the faster the pulse.
